title: Research Capstone — A Falsifiable World-Model Study
learningQuestion: What must happen between “HYPOTHESIS” and “BOUNDARY” in Research Capstone — A Falsifiable World-Model Study?
depiction: It is a wind-tunnel experiment: small enough to inspect, strict enough to reject an attractive but wrong design. Where the analogy breaks: a course-scale result can validate a mechanism and workflow but cannot establish production performance or a field-wide conclusion. The composition follows HYPOTHESIS → PROTOCOL → EVIDENCE → BOUNDARY and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of Research Capstone — A Falsifiable World-Model Study: The final capstone asks you to choose one specialization and produce a small, reproducible study whose claim could genuinely fail.
longDescription: It is a wind-tunnel experiment: small enough to inspect, strict enough to reject an attractive but wrong design. Where the analogy breaks: a course-scale result can validate a mechanism and workflow but cannot establish production performance or a field-wide conclusion. The composition follows HYPOTHESIS → PROTOCOL → EVIDENCE → BOUNDARY and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: Choose the geometry branch. Here `rotation-equivariant` means rotating input position/action by $R$ should rotate the predicted velocity by the same $R$: $f(Rx,Ra)=Rf(x,a)$. Preregister: with four training angles, four held-out rotations, seeds [1,2,3], and 200 matched updates, the equivariant rule is supported only if mean held-out vector error improves by at least 0.05 over lookup and every equation residual is below 0.02. Raw fixture results are errors 0.12 versus 0.13 (improvement 0.01) and maximum residual 0.018, so the primary improvement threshold fails: report the hypothesis as not supported despite passing the equation-residual gate.
boundary: A capstone is not complete when a demo runs; it needs a committed question, baseline, falsification rule, changed-case test, and evidence boundary.
stageDescriptions: Write a claim and threshold that can fail.
Pin the matched fixture, intervention, baseline, and checks.
Preserve raw results, failures, uncertainty, and artifacts.
Apply the rule, state scope, and propose a discriminating next test.

title: What Is a Generative Model?
learningQuestion: What must happen between “QUESTION” and “LIMIT” in What Is a Generative Model??
depiction: Think of a musician who has learned a style well enough to improvise a new melody on request. Where the analogy breaks: a model has no lived musical intention, and an apparently new output may still copy, omit, or distort patterns from its data. The composition follows QUESTION → EXAMPLE → COMPARE → LIMIT and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of What Is a Generative Model?: A generative model learns patterns from examples so it can create a new example, such as text, an image, audio, or an action.
longDescription: Think of a musician who has learned a style well enough to improvise a new melody on request. Where the analogy breaks: a model has no lived musical intention, and an apparently new output may still copy, omit, or distort patterns from its data. The composition follows QUESTION → EXAMPLE → COMPARE → LIMIT and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: A sticker generator receives ‘a cheerful blue robot waving.’ Output A shows a round robot; output B shows a square robot with a different pose; output C is a blue car. A and B demonstrate relevant variation. C demonstrates variety without following the request, so more difference is not automatically better generation.
boundary: One striking output does not show that a generator is consistently relevant, varied, original, or representative. Quality belongs to a set of outputs and a declared use, not only to the best example.
stageDescriptions: Name what kind of thing should be created and what request should guide it.
Generate more than one result so variation becomes visible instead of assumed.
Compare relevance, variety, missing cases, and suspicious copying before judging quality.
These cards are hand-authored evaluation fixtures, not outputs from a trained image model.

title: Likelihood, Cross-Entropy & Units
learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in Likelihood, Cross-Entropy & Units?
depiction: Likelihood is a receipt listing how surprised the model was by every observed event; logs let the surprises add. Where the analogy breaks: the receipt scores observations under model assumptions and does not alone judge perceptual quality or usefulness. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of Likelihood, Cross-Entropy & Units: Likelihood asks how much probability a model assigns to observed data; cross-entropy averages the surprise produced when a target distribution is scored by the model distribution.
longDescription: Likelihood is a receipt listing how surprised the model was by every observed event; logs let the surprises add. Where the analogy breaks: the receipt scores observations under model assumptions and does not alone judge perceptual quality or usefulness. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: If a length-three sequence receives conditional probabilities $[0.8,0.5,0.25]$, its probability is 0.1 and NLL is $2.303$ nats, equal to $0.223+0.693+1.386$. Separately, for target $p=[0.75,0.25]$ and model $q=[0.60,0.40]$, $H(p,q)=-(0.75\log0.60+0.25\log0.40)\approx0.612$ nats. A dataset with three A labels and one B label has the same empirical mean NLL under q.
boundary: A smaller average negative log-likelihood does not guarantee more diverse or more preferred samples, especially across different representations or model families.
stageDescriptions: Use logs to replace fragile probability products with stable additive terms.
Name the reduction unit—example, token, dimension, nat, or bit—before comparing values.
Inspect per-case contributions so an average cannot hide rare catastrophic assignments.
The fixture checks measurement compatibility, not whether likelihood predicts human preference.

title: Sampling, Seeds & Random Variables
learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in Sampling, Seeds & Random Variables?
depiction: A seed is the starting page of a very long prewritten book of random-looking numbers. Where the analogy breaks: reopening the same page repeats a path but says nothing about how typical that path is across the full distribution. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of Sampling, Seeds & Random Variables: Sampling uses a controlled random variable to choose one outcome from a model distribution; a seed makes the random stream repeatable, not representative.
longDescription: A seed is the starting page of a very long prewritten book of random-looking numbers. Where the analogy breaks: reopening the same page repeats a path but says nothing about how typical that path is across the full distribution. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: With cumulative mass [0.2,0.7,1.0], draws [0.12,0.64,0.91] select [A,B,C]. In an autoregressive model each selection changes the next conditional distribution, so one changed draw can alter the entire continuation.
boundary: Running one favorable seed repeatedly is not replication and cannot estimate average performance, variance, rare failures, or sample diversity.
stageDescriptions: Treat randomness as an explicit input to the program and artifact.
Separate repeatability of one path from coverage across independent seeds.
Record sampling order and stopping rules because they consume the random stream.
This plans random streams; it does not execute stochastic training or estimate real variance.

title: KL, Divergences & Distance
learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in KL, Divergences & Distance?
depiction: Divergences are different lenses for comparing two maps of the same territory. Where the analogy breaks: a lens encodes priorities; no single scalar captures every fidelity, coverage, and geometry concern. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of KL, Divergences & Distance: A divergence compares two distributions by a declared rule; different rules emphasize different mistakes and need not agree.
longDescription: Divergences are different lenses for comparing two maps of the same territory. Where the analogy breaks: a lens encodes priorities; no single scalar captures every fidelity, coverage, and geometry concern. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: For $p=[0.5,0.5]$ and $q=[0.9,0.1]$, $D_{KL}(p\|q)\approx0.511$ nats while $D_{KL}(q\|p)\approx0.368$; swapping arguments changes the weighting.
boundary: Calling a value distance does not make it symmetric, bounded, or meaningful across different representations and sample spaces.
stageDescriptions: Write the argument order because forward and reverse KL answer different questions.
Check support zeros before computing logarithms or interpreting infinity.
Choose a comparison whose geometry and failure emphasis match the decision.
Values use two-outcome arithmetic and do not estimate high-dimensional sample quality.

title: Build a Distribution Workbench
learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in Build a Distribution Workbench?
depiction: It is a wind tunnel for probability mechanisms: small enough to inspect, instrumented enough to reveal failure. Where the analogy breaks: passing finite categorical tests does not validate a neural density estimator or perceptual sample metric. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of Build a Distribution Workbench: A distribution workbench is a tested package that represents finite distributions, samples reproducibly, scores observations, and compares changed cases.
longDescription: It is a wind tunnel for probability mechanisms: small enough to inspect, instrumented enough to reveal failure. Where the analogy breaks: passing finite categorical tests does not validate a neural density estimator or perceptual sample metric. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: For p=[0.2,0.5,0.3], 10,000 seeded samples should contain only declared labels and frequencies near p within a predeclared tolerance; exact equality is not an invariant. Replaying the same seed and call order must match exactly.
boundary: A polished histogram is not a correctness suite; sampling, likelihood, units, support handling, and seed behavior need independent assertions.
stageDescriptions: Build one narrow interface that makes every assumption inspectable.
Test invariants separately from stochastic observations and convergence bands.
Preserve failing seeds and changed distributions as first-class evidence.
The cards specify tests; no browser random experiment is presented as a measurement.

title: Autoregressive Generators Beyond Text
learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in Autoregressive Generators Beyond Text?
depiction: It resembles completing a mosaic while covering every tile you have not reached yet. Where the analogy breaks: the chosen traversal can privilege some spatial dependencies and slow sampling even when training is parallel. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of Autoregressive Generators Beyond Text: An autoregressive generator creates a structured example one ordered variable at a time, conditioning each choice on everything already generated.
longDescription: It resembles completing a mosaic while covering every tile you have not reached yet. Where the analogy breaks: the chosen traversal can privilege some spatial dependencies and slow sampling even when training is parallel. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: A 2×2 binary image serialized [top-left, top-right, bottom-left, bottom-right] contributes four factors. When predicting bottom-left, only the two top pixels may be visible; changing bottom-right must not change that earlier logit.
boundary: Autoregressive does not mean text-only or recurrent-network-only; it describes a probability factorization that Transformers, convolutions, or tables can implement.
stageDescriptions: Declare the serialization before constructing causal inputs and targets.
Use a causal mask so no prediction reads the variable it must predict or any later variable.
Evaluate both log-likelihood and order-sensitive sampling cost or failure.
This is a four-variable dependency fixture, not a trained image model.

title: Latent-Variable Models
learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in Latent-Variable Models?
depiction: A latent is an unseen recipe choice that explains why the finished dish has its observed properties. Where the analogy breaks: the model may invent entangled or non-identifiable causes that do not correspond to human concepts. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of Latent-Variable Models: A latent-variable model explains an observation using an unobserved variable that captures hidden causes or compact factors.
longDescription: A latent is an unseen recipe choice that explains why the finished dish has its observed properties. Where the analogy breaks: the model may invent entangled or non-identifiable causes that do not correspond to human concepts. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: A binary latent z chooses fair coin A or biased coin B with prior [0.5,0.5]. If heads likelihoods are [0.5,0.9], marginal P(heads)=0.7 and posterior P(B|heads)=0.45/0.7≈0.643.
boundary: A low-dimensional latent is not automatically disentangled, causal, identifiable, or semantically named.
stageDescriptions: Separate the generative direction prior→latent→observation from posterior inference.
Marginalize hidden alternatives rather than selecting one explanation prematurely.
Intervene on latent values to test what the decoder actually uses.
A two-latent Bayes table demonstrates inference arithmetic, not neural representation quality.

title: Amortized Inference & the ELBO
learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in Amortized Inference & the ELBO?
depiction: The encoder is a clerk who learns to propose likely hidden explanations without solving a fresh search for every case. Where the analogy breaks: fast proposals can be systematically biased, and a tight bound is not guaranteed by fluent reconstructions. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of Amortized Inference & the ELBO: Amortized inference trains one encoder to quickly approximate a posterior for many observations; the ELBO balances reconstruction with closeness to a prior.
longDescription: The encoder is a clerk who learns to propose likely hidden explanations without solving a fresh search for every case. Where the analogy breaks: fast proposals can be systematically biased, and a tight bound is not guaranteed by fluent reconstructions. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: If expected reconstruction log-likelihood is −80 nats and KL(q||p)=5 nats, ELBO is −85 nats and negative ELBO loss is 85. Raising KL weight to 2 would optimize 90, a different objective rather than a tighter estimate.
boundary: The ELBO is not the exact log-likelihood unless the approximate posterior equals the true posterior; changing term weights changes the objective's probabilistic interpretation.
stageDescriptions: Keep reconstruction and KL terms separate in the training ledger.
Use reparameterization to expose randomness while retaining a differentiable path.
Interpret the ELBO as a bound with an approximation gap, not exact evidence.
Fixed terms illustrate bookkeeping; no encoder or decoder is trained in the browser.

title: VAEs & Posterior Collapse
learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in VAEs & Posterior Collapse?
depiction: The encoder keeps writing notes, but the decoder has learned to finish the task without reading them. Where the analogy breaks: a small KL can also be appropriate for simple data; collapse is a mechanism diagnosis, not a threshold alone. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of VAEs & Posterior Collapse: Posterior collapse occurs when a VAE's approximate posterior stays near the prior and the decoder learns to ignore the latent variable.
longDescription: The encoder keeps writing notes, but the decoder has learned to finish the task without reading them. Where the analogy breaks: a small KL can also be appropriate for simple data; collapse is a mechanism diagnosis, not a threshold alone. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. title: Change of Variables
learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in Change of Variables?
depiction: Ink density thins when a rubber sheet stretches and thickens when the sheet compresses. Where the analogy breaks: high-dimensional invertibility and determinant computation constrain which neural transformations are practical. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of Change of Variables: Change of variables moves probability density through an invertible transformation while correcting for how the transformation stretches space.
longDescription: Ink density thins when a rubber sheet stretches and thickens when the sheet compresses. Where the analogy breaks: high-dimensional invertibility and determinant computation constrain which neural transformations are practical. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: Let z be uniform on [0,1] and x=2z. The transformed support is [0,2], inverse is z=x/2, and inverse derivative is 1/2, so p_X(x)=1/2 on [0,2] and integrates to one.
boundary: Applying an invertible neural network to samples does not define the correct density unless the Jacobian correction is included and tractable.
stageDescriptions: Trace both the transformed point and the local volume correction.
Use absolute determinant because orientation flips do not create negative density.
Check invertibility and normalization independently of visual plausibility.
This one-dimensional uniform fixture does not expose high-dimensional Jacobian cost.

title: Normalizing Flows
learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in Normalizing Flows?
depiction: A flow repeatedly folds and stretches a simple sheet while keeping a reversible map of every move. Where the analogy breaks: requiring a reversible path can make some topology changes or discrete data interfaces awkward. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of Normalizing Flows: A normalizing flow composes invertible transformations so a simple base distribution becomes a flexible distribution with exact sampling and density evaluation.
longDescription: A flow repeatedly folds and stretches a simple sheet while keeping a reversible map of every move. Where the analogy breaks: requiring a reversible path can make some topology changes or discrete data interfaces awkward. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: An affine coupling keeps x1=z1 and sets x2=z2 exp(s(z1))+t(z1). The inverse recovers z2=(x2−t(x1))exp(−s(x1)); log determinant is s(z1), avoiding a full matrix determinant.
boundary: Exact likelihood does not mean the learned density prioritizes perceptually or semantically important structure.
stageDescriptions: Implement forward, inverse, and log determinant as one inseparable layer contract.
Compose log determinants in the same order as transformations.
Test round-trip error and density against an analytically solvable case before training.
These are deterministic layer traces, not a trained flow benchmark.

title: Energy-Based Models
learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in Energy-Based Models?
depiction: A landscape lets a marble settle in low valleys that represent preferred states. Where the analogy breaks: deep or numerous valleys can trap a sampler, and valley depth alone does not reveal normalized probability mass. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of Energy-Based Models: An energy-based model assigns low energy to preferred configurations and high energy to unlikely ones, defining probability only after normalization.
longDescription: A landscape lets a marble settle in low valleys that represent preferred states. Where the analogy breaks: deep or numerous valleys can trap a sampler, and valley depth alone does not reveal normalized probability mass. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: For energies [0,1,2], unnormalized weights are [1,.368,.135], Z≈1.503, and probabilities ≈[.665,.245,.090]. Adding 10 to every energy changes Z but not normalized probabilities.
boundary: A model that gives data lower energy than one negative batch is not necessarily normalized, well sampled, or robust to unseen low-energy regions.
stageDescriptions: Distinguish unnormalized energy differences from normalized probabilities.
Keep positive data examples and negative model samples visible in the gradient ledger.
Audit whether the sampler explores modes before trusting training loss.
Finite tables make Z exact; real continuous EBMs may require approximate sampling.

title: MCMC & Langevin Sampling
learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in MCMC & Langevin Sampling?
depiction: A noisy hiker tends downhill but receives random shoves that can escape shallow valleys. Where the analogy breaks: a finite chain can remain trapped, and correlated states are not equivalent to independent samples. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of MCMC & Langevin Sampling: MCMC builds a chain whose long-run state distribution approaches a target; Langevin sampling uses energy gradients plus noise to move toward low-energy regions while still exploring.
longDescription: A noisy hiker tends downhill but receives random shoves that can escape shallow valleys. Where the analogy breaks: a finite chain can remain trapped, and correlated states are not equivalent to independent samples. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: For the two-state kernel $P=[[0.8,0.2],[0.4,0.6]]$, $\pi=[2/3,1/3]$ satisfies $\pi P=[2/3,1/3]$; positive cross-state and self-transition probabilities make this finite chain irreducible and aperiodic. For a standard-normal MALA step from $x=0$ with $\epsilon=0.5$ and proposed $y=2$, the forward log-proposal term (up to a shared constant) is −4, the reverse is −2.25, and $\log\pi(y)-\log\pi(x)=-2$. Thus log acceptance ratio is −0.25, $\alpha\approx0.779$; draw $u=0.90$ rejects and the chain remains at 0.
boundary: More chain steps do not guarantee independent or representative samples when modes are separated or the discretization is biased.
stageDescriptions: Trace drift and noise as separate update contributions.
Evaluate chain mixing and mode occupancy rather than plotting only the final state.
Report step size, burn-in, thinning policy, starts, and transition budget.
The readouts are teaching fixtures, not measured MCMC diagnostics.

alt: Visual explanation of Flows vs Energies: Sampling Lab: This capstone builds a two-dimensional target distribution, an invertible flow, and an energy sampler, then compares exact-density and iterative-sampling trade-offs under matched evidence.

learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in Corruption & Denoising?

The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of Corruption & Denoising: Diffusion training deliberately corrupts data with known noise, then learns information needed to reverse that corruption step by step.
longDescription: It is like adding measured layers of fog to photographs and training a restorer who knows the fog level. Where the analogy breaks: denoising predicts a distributional reverse step, not the unique original behind a noisy observation. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: For scalar x0=2, alpha_bar=.64, and epsilon=−1, xt=.8×2+.6×(−1)=1.0. The same x0 and timestep produce different xt under another epsilon.
boundary: The model does not learn to add noise, and a noisy input does not contain a uniquely recoverable clean example at high corruption.
stageDescriptions: Keep clean data, sampled noise, timestep, and corrupted input in the training record.
Interpret schedules through signal-to-noise, not timestep number alone.
Distinguish the fixed forward process from learned reverse dynamics.

title: Scores & Denoising Score Matching
learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in Scores & Denoising Score Matching?
depiction: The score is a compass that points uphill on a log-density landscape. Where the analogy breaks: local directions may be inaccurate far from data, and following a compass requires a step rule and noise model. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of Scores & Denoising Score Matching: A score is the gradient of log density with respect to the data; it points toward locally higher-density regions without requiring the normalization constant.
longDescription: The score is a compass that points uphill on a log-density landscape. Where the analogy breaks: local directions may be inaccurate far from data, and following a compass requires a step rule and noise model. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: For standard normal p, score at x=2 is −2. For one denoising target, let $x_0=2$, $\sigma_t=.5$, and $\epsilon=-1$, so $x_t=1.5$. The conditional target is $-(1.5-2)/.25=+2$, equivalently $-\epsilon/\sigma_t$. If the network predicts 1.4, unweighted squared error is $(1.4-2)^2=.36$ and $\sigma_t^2$ weighting contributes $.25\times.36=.09$.
boundary: A score is not a quality rating and does not directly give normalized density or a generated sample.
stageDescriptions: Distinguish scalar log density from its vector gradient field.
Check analytic scores on simple distributions before fitting a network.
Evaluate direction, magnitude, and trajectory consequences across noise levels.
These one-dimensional fields are deterministic analytic fixtures, not learned high-dimensional score measurements.

title: The DDPM Training Objective
learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in The DDPM Training Objective?
depiction: The network receives a fogged image plus the fog setting and must identify the exact fog pattern that was added. Where the analogy breaks: accurate average noise prediction does not uniquely reconstruct every clean sample or guarantee good iterative sampling. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of The DDPM Training Objective: The common DDPM objective trains a network to predict the exact noise used to corrupt a clean example at a sampled timestep.
longDescription: The network receives a fogged image plus the fog setting and must identify the exact fog pattern that was added. Where the analogy breaks: accurate average noise prediction does not uniquely reconstruct every clean sample or guarantee good iterative sampling. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: For scalar epsilon=−1.2 and prediction −0.9, squared error is .09. If the batch mean mixes easy low-noise and hard high-noise cases, slice losses by t before interpreting progress.
boundary: The network target is not always the clean image; calling every diffusion loss reconstruction loss hides the chosen parameterization.
stageDescriptions: Record x0, t, epsilon, xt, prediction, and reduction together.
Pass timestep information explicitly so one network represents many noise levels.
Test target parameterization and weighting with algebraic conversions.

title: Diffusion Samplers & Schedules
learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in Diffusion Samplers & Schedules?
depiction: A sampler chooses the route and stride length for descending from noise toward data using the learned direction field. Where the analogy breaks: shortcuts can leave the learned field's accurate region and a deterministic route may reduce output variation for fixed starting noise. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of Diffusion Samplers & Schedules: A diffusion sampler discretizes the learned reverse path; fewer steps reduce compute but change error accumulation, stochasticity, and sometimes sample diversity.
longDescription: A sampler chooses the route and stride length for descending from noise toward data using the learned direction field. Where the analogy breaks: shortcuts can leave the learned field's accurate region and a deterministic route may reduce output variation for fixed starting noise. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: Trace one scalar deterministic DDIM step. Let $\bar\alpha_t=.64$, $\bar\alpha_{prev}=.81$, $x_t=.88$, and predicted noise $\hat\epsilon=-.9$. First $\hat{x}_0=(x_t-\sqrt{1-.64}\hat\epsilon)/\sqrt{.64}=(.88+.54)/.8=1.775$. With $\eta=0$, $x_{prev}=\sqrt{.81}\hat{x}_0+\sqrt{1-.81}\hat\epsilon=.9(1.775)+\sqrt{.19}(-.9)\approx1.205$. A stochastic setting adds a schedule-derived $\sigma_tz$ term and reduces the coefficient on the predicted-noise direction accordingly.
boundary: A sampler with fewer steps is not automatically faster on every device or equivalent in quality; kernel overhead, batching, and model calls matter.
stageDescriptions: Count network function evaluations and stochastic draws explicitly.
Hold starting noises and model weights fixed when comparing trajectories.
Inspect intermediate states and failure onset, not only final image grids.
The cards specify sampler arithmetic and experiments; no runtime or sample-quality number is measured here.

longDescription: It is a recipe generator that receives dietary constraints before proposing dishes. alt: Visual explanation of Classifier-Free Guidance: Classifier-free guidance combines conditional and unconditional denoiser predictions to strengthen condition influence without a separate classifier.

Where the analogy breaks: extrapolation can leave the region learned during training and does not guarantee faithful semantics. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: For scalar epsilon_u=2 and epsilon_c=1, w=3 gives epsilon_cfg=2+3(−1)=−1. The extrapolated value lies beyond both predictions, showing why large guidance can introduce artifacts.

title: Inverse Problems & Editing

alt: Visual explanation of Inverse Problems & Editing: An inverse problem infers a plausible complete example from incomplete or noisy measurements while enforcing consistency with what was actually observed.

title: Data & Systems for Generative Models
learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in Data & Systems for Generative Models?
depiction: It is a scientific production line where every material lot, machine setting, and interrupted run is traceable. Where the analogy breaks: high throughput and exact resume establish systems behavior, not statistical validity or model quality. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of Data & Systems for Generative Models: A generative training system turns versioned data and randomness into checkpoints, logs, and samples under explicit compute, memory, and provenance constraints.
longDescription: It is a scientific production line where every material lot, machine setting, and interrupted run is traceable. Where the analogy breaks: high throughput and exact resume establish systems behavior, not statistical validity or model quality. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: A 100M-parameter bf16 training fixture uses 0.2 GB weights, 0.2 GB gradients, and fp32 Adam state consisting of a 0.4 GB master copy plus 0.4 GB each for first and second moments. With 1.6 GB peak activations, the declared ledger totals 3.2 GB before allocator/workspace overhead; doubling batch-linked activations raises it to 4.8 GB and exceeds a 4 GB device. Processing 200,000 pixels in 4 seconds is 50,000 pixels/s for that exact shape and hardware. Separately, a checkpoint that omits EMA and data cursor loads but evaluates a different parameter copy and repeats examples. An immutable dataset snapshot ID plus rights/license record distinguishes that trajectory failure from a provenance failure.
boundary: A fixed seed and saved weights alone are not a reproducible training run.
stageDescriptions: Version data and preprocessing as carefully as code and checkpoints.
Name ownership and units for every performance or memory measurement.
Test interruption and resume before committing a long GPU budget.
These are deterministic metadata and arithmetic fixtures; no GPU memory or training trajectory is measured in the browser.

title: Reproducible GPU Experiments
learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in Reproducible GPU Experiments?
depiction: It is a sealed field kit with a manifest, calibration checks, sample containers, and an abort checklist. Where the analogy breaks: a reproducible procedure can reproduce a flawed experiment, and cloud hardware may still introduce nondeterminism. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of Reproducible GPU Experiments: A reproducible GPU experiment packages a portable command, pinned environment, bounded budgets, expected invariants, variable observations, explicit checkpoint/resume status, raw logs, and failure diagnostics.
longDescription: It is a sealed field kit with a manifest, calibration checks, sample containers, and an abort checklist. Where the analogy breaks: a reproducible procedure can reproduce a flawed experiment, and cloud hardware may still introduce nondeterminism. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: The smoke profile runs 25 optimization steps, verifies paired initialization, equal loss-bearing-example budgets, finite loss and sample coordinates, eight mode-count fields, and a valid JSON dossier, then stops. Its dossier records source/runner revisions, resolved hardware, 20 batched denoiser invocations over 128 examples (2,560 per-example evaluations), and an explicit checkpoint/resume status. It does not create a checkpoint or claim loss convergence or recognizable quality. The full profile uses the declared three-seed set and the same artifact schema.
boundary: A Colab notebook is not reproducible merely because it has ordered cells; package drift, hidden state, downloaded data, hardware, and unbounded reruns must be controlled.
stageDescriptions: Use one provider-neutral command and externalize only environment setup.
Separate smoke integration from full experimental evidence and enforce stop limits.
Never publish a numeric expected band without a reviewed provenance-backed run.
No external service is contacted by this browser lab.

title: What Is Reinforcement Learning?
learningQuestion: What must happen between “QUESTION” and “LIMIT” in What Is Reinforcement Learning??
depiction: Think of learning a game by playing, noticing what each move leads to, and changing your strategy on the next round. Where the analogy breaks: Feedback teaches exactly the behavior it rewards. If points favor speed while the real goal also requires safety, an agent can learn a fast but unacceptable shortcut. The composition follows QUESTION → EXAMPLE → COMPARE → LIMIT and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of What Is Reinforcement Learning?: Reinforcement learning is a way for an agent to improve its choices by trying actions, observing consequences, and using that feedback on later attempts.
longDescription: Think of learning a game by playing, noticing what each move leads to, and changing your strategy on the next round. Where the analogy breaks: Feedback teaches exactly the behavior it rewards. If points favor speed while the real goal also requires safety, an agent can learn a fast but unacceptable shortcut. The composition follows QUESTION → EXAMPLE → COMPARE → LIMIT and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: A maze agent first turns left and reaches a dead end. On the next attempt it turns right, finds the exit, and records that this choice worked better in that situation. Repeating the loop can improve its route. It still needs separate checks for collisions or forbidden shortcuts, because reaching the exit is not the only outcome that matters.
boundary: Feedback teaches exactly the behavior it rewards. If points favor speed while the real goal also requires safety, an agent can learn a fast but unacceptable shortcut.

title: MDPs, Rewards & Discounting
learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in MDPs, Rewards & Discounting?

Worked reference: From safe, action fast reaches goal with probability .8 for reward 5 and crash with .2 for reward −20; expected immediate reward is $0.8(5)+0.2(-20)=0$, hiding the severe outcome distribution. For a separate three-step reward trace [0,0,10], $G_0$ is $.5^2(10)=2.5$ at $\gamma=.5$ but $.9^2(10)=8.1$ at $\gamma=.9$: the outcomes did not change, only the weight assigned to delay.

title: Deep Q-Networks
learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in Deep Q-Networks?
depiction: One network scores every available lever; replay supplies old consequences and the delayed copy supplies a steadier next-score target. alt: Visual explanation of Deep Q-Networks: A deep Q-network predicts an action value for every discrete action and trains those predictions from replayed one-step targets.
longDescription: One network scores every available lever; replay supplies old consequences and the delayed copy supplies a steadier next-score target. Where the analogy breaks: A decreasing DQN loss can accompany Q overestimation, replay collapse, insufficient exploration, or worse policy return. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: One complete step collects (s,action 1,reward 1,s',not-terminal), appends it to replay, samples a batch [B,·], gathers Q(s,a) from [B,A], builds delayed targets, reduces Huber loss, updates online weights, conditionally copies target weights, then evaluates with epsilon zero and no updates.
boundary: A decreasing DQN loss can accompany Q overestimation, replay collapse, insufficient exploration, or worse policy return.
stageDescriptions: Assert observation, action, reward, ending, and Q tensor shapes.

title: Exploration in Deep RL
learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in Exploration in Deep RL?

alt: Visual explanation of Exploration in Deep RL: Exploration chooses actions that may reveal useful outcomes instead of always exploiting current value estimates.
longDescription: Exploration is an information budget: spend risky or costly actions only when their possible learning value justifies them. Where the analogy breaks: More coverage is not automatically better when exploration violates constraints or visits irrelevant regions. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: Epsilon-greedy picks random actions without state knowledge; count scores start=.1 and door=.5, so bonus favors door. An ensemble predicts door values [2,6,4] versus start [3,3.2,2.8], so higher disagreement also selects door while exposing calibration risk.
boundary: More coverage is not automatically better when exploration violates constraints or visits irrelevant regions.

The cards are deterministic comparison scenarios rather than executed agents.

Worked reference: The behavior policy chose the sampled action with probability .4; the updated policy [.6,.4] assigns that same action probability .6. The ratio is $.6/.4=1.5$, clipped to 1.2, so the positive-advantage surrogate is $1.2(2)=2.4$. Critic .6 versus target 1 gives squared loss .16, and updated-policy entropy is .673. With value coefficient .5 and entropy .01, total loss is $-2.4+.5(.16)-.01(.673)=-2.3267$.

title: Shooting, CEM & Model Predictive Control

alt: Visual explanation of Shooting, CEM & Model Predictive Control: Shooting planners score complete candidate action sequences in a model; model predictive control executes only the first action and replans after a new observation.
longDescription: Draft several routes, refine the promising ones, take one step, then redraw the map from what actually happened. Where the analogy breaks: More search can worsen real behavior when it exploits a biased model or overfits noisy predicted rewards. Worked reference: Random shooting scores sequences [−1,+1]→1, [+1,+1]→4, [+1,−1]→2. CEM keeps the top two, whose per-step action mean refits to [+1,0], resamples around that proposal, and repeats; 100×10×4 costs 4,000 predicted transitions.

title: Behavior Cloning
learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in Behavior Cloning?
depiction: Learn a driver's action choices from recorded views, knowing the learned driver may later create views absent from the recordings. Where the analogy breaks: Low supervised loss does not establish closed-loop control because small errors change future observations and can compound off data support. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of Behavior Cloning: Behavior cloning trains a policy to predict the action demonstrated in each recorded state or observation.
longDescription: Learn a driver's action choices from recorded views, knowing the learned driver may later create views absent from the recordings. Where the analogy breaks: Low supervised loss does not establish closed-loop control because small errors change future observations and can compound off data support. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: For logits [0,0] and demonstrated turn (class 1), softmax is [.5,.5], cross-entropy is −log(.5)=.693, gradient p−y=[.5,−.5], and learning rate .1 updates logits to [−.05,.05], raising turn probability.
boundary: Low supervised loss does not establish closed-loop control because small errors change future observations and can compound off data support.
stageDescriptions: Split by episode or scenario before creating overlapping windows.
Trace observation and action shapes, units, masks, and normalization.
Evaluate closed-loop recovery in addition to held-out imitation loss.
The percentages are deterministic teaching scenarios, not policy measurements.

title: Covariate Shift & Dataset Aggregation
learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in Covariate Shift & Dataset Aggregation?
depiction: Let the student drive, pause at the situations it creates, and ask the instructor what should have happened there. Where the analogy breaks: DAgger can be unsafe or expensive when learner mistakes cannot be tolerated or expert labels are inconsistent. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of Covariate Shift & Dataset Aggregation: DAgger reduces imitation covariate shift by running the learner, asking an expert for the correct action on learner-visited states, and aggregating those labeled states into training data.
longDescription: Let the student drive, pause at the situations it creates, and ask the instructor what should have happened there. Where the analogy breaks: DAgger can be unsafe or expensive when learner mistakes cannot be tolerated or expert labels are inconsistent. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: D0 has 100 nominal rows and no recovery states. Learner iteration 1 visits 40 recovery states; expert labels 35 right and 5 stop. Aggregate D1=D0∪40 rows, retrain policy pi1, then a held-out recovery rollout chooses right in 8/10 cases versus pi0's 0/10.

stageDescriptions: Keep executed action separate from expert label.
Track learner-visited state coverage and expert query provenance by iteration.
Define safety shields and stop authority before learner rollouts.
The counts are design fixtures, not real expert interactions.

title: Offline RL, Coverage & Conservatism
learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in Offline RL, Coverage & Conservatism?
depiction: Choose a route using only old travel logs; roads never taken cannot acquire trustworthy outcomes just because a model scores them highly. Where the analogy breaks: Conservatism reduces some unsupported optimism but cannot recover absent causal outcomes or guarantee safe policy improvement. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of Offline RL, Coverage & Conservatism: Offline reinforcement learning improves a policy using a fixed logged dataset without collecting corrective transitions during training.
longDescription: Choose a route using only old travel logs; roads never taken cannot acquire trustworthy outcomes just because a model scores them highly. Where the analogy breaks: Conservatism reduces some unsupported optimism but cannot recover absent causal outcomes or guarantee safe policy improvement. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: At state s, behavior action counts are [100,0], while Q estimates are [5,20]. With explicit zero-based labels, action index 1 is the second action and has zero logged support; greedily selecting it is an extrapolation claim, not logged evidence.
boundary: Conservatism reduces some unsupported optimism but cannot recover absent causal outcomes or guarantee safe policy improvement.
stageDescriptions: Audit critical state-action coverage before optimization.
Compare behavior cloning, value learning, and conservative variants on the same logs.
Abstain where target actions have no credible support or validated model evidence.

title: Sequence Policies from Logged Data
learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in Sequence Policies from Logged Data?
depiction: Turn each logged episode into a causal decision notebook while keeping every page tied to the behavior that produced it. Where the analogy breaks: Conditioning on a high desired return does not create causal knowledge of unsupported actions or prove online performance. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of Sequence Policies from Logged Data: This capstone builds a trajectory-sequence policy from logged data and audits whether sequence modeling improves decisions without escaping dataset support.
longDescription: Turn each logged episode into a causal decision notebook while keeping every page tied to the behavior that produced it. Where the analogy breaks: Conditioning on a high desired return does not create causal knowledge of unsupported actions or prove online performance. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: For one episode, serialize `[R0,s0,a0,R1,s1,a1]`. The logit at state position `s0` predicts target `a0` and may read only `[R0,s0]`; the logit at `s1` predicts `a1` and may read `[R0,s0,a0,R1,s1]`. With batch 2, six token positions, width 32, and two actions, token states have shape `[2,6,32]`, the broadcast causal-plus-padding mask is `[2,1,6,6]`, and action logits are `[2,6,2]` before selecting state positions. Replacing future `a1` must leave the earlier `a0` logit unchanged. After this leakage check, if high-return context still chooses unseen action B in a critical state, a support guard routes to logged action A and records abstention rather than claiming high-return control.
boundary: Conditioning on a high desired return does not create causal knowledge of unsupported actions or prove online performance.
stageDescriptions: Test serialization, target shifting, masks, and episode boundaries exactly.
Compare with behavior cloning under the same data and update budget.
Treat desired return as context, not a guarantee of achieving it.
The checklist does not train or evaluate a real sequence model.

title: RL Evaluation, Seeds & Uncertainty
learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in RL Evaluation, Seeds & Uncertainty?
depiction: Each seed is a complete laboratory replicate with its own learning history, not one dot to hide inside a pooled episode average. Where the analogy breaks: Five seeds and one environment give bounded uncertainty; they do not establish universal algorithm rankings. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of RL Evaluation, Seeds & Uncertainty: Reliable RL evaluation treats a complete seeded training run as the main experimental unit and preserves learning, evaluation, and failure trajectories instead of one final average.
longDescription: Each seed is a complete laboratory replicate with its own learning history, not one dot to hide inside a pooled episode average. Where the analogy breaks: Five seeds and one environment give bounded uncertainty; they do not establish universal algorithm rankings. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: At identical 100k interactions, 10k updates, and 100 evaluation episodes per seed, A returns [80,82,78,81,20] and B [75,77,76,78,74]. Paired effects [5,5,2,3,−54] retain the failed A seed rather than pooling 500 episodes per arm.
boundary: Five seeds and one environment give bounded uncertainty; they do not establish universal algorithm rankings.
stageDescriptions: Predeclare seed count, primary metric, window, stop rule, and failure handling.
Separate training return from frozen-policy evaluation episodes.
Show individual curves and uncertainty at the independent-run level.
The returns are fixed teaching rows, not benchmark measurements.

longDescription: Choose the best route inside a fence; do not convert every fence into a small toll the agent can pay for higher reward. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: Policy A return 100 with cost .12 violates limit .05; B return 85 with cost .03 is feasible. A cannot win by reward under the hard decision rule.

title: Reproducible GPU RL
learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in Reproducible GPU RL?
depiction: Package the whole learning experiment—not merely the neural network—as a bounded field kit another learner can inspect or rerun. Where the analogy breaks: Provider reproducibility does not guarantee bitwise GPU determinism, correct science, or transfer beyond the pinned environment and revision. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of Reproducible GPU RL: A reproducible RL accelerator run pins the environment, agent, budgets, seeds, evaluation, and artifact schema behind one portable smoke/full command.
longDescription: Package the whole learning experiment—not merely the neural network—as a bounded field kit another learner can inspect or rerun. Where the analogy breaks: Provider reproducibility does not guarantee bitwise GPU determinism, correct science, or transfer beyond the pinned environment and revision. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: The documented smoke command runs seed 11 for 200 environment steps per target-copy arm and eight frozen-policy evaluation episodes. After 64-transition replay warmup, both arms perform the same 137 optimizer updates; interval 20 copies six times and interval 100 copies once, so smoke exercises the treatment. Integer states 0–4 become one-hot input `[B,5]`; Q output is `[B,2]`. Exact checks require paired initialization, 200 matched steps, matched updates, at least one copy per arm, finite loss/Q/return values, revision plus hardware provenance, and the declared schema. It does not promise reward improvement. Full uses seeds 11/23/41/53/67, 20,000 steps per arm, and 100 evaluation episodes per seed.
boundary: Provider reproducibility does not guarantee bitwise GPU determinism, correct science, or transfer beyond the pinned environment and revision.
stageDescriptions: Use one provider-neutral command for Colab, compatible services, and local execution.
Separate smoke integration from full seed-level evidence.
Publish expected observations only from exact invariants or reviewed authentic runs.
No external service is contacted and no benchmark number is measured here.

title: What Is Embodied AI?
learningQuestion: What must happen between “QUESTION” and “LIMIT” in What Is Embodied AI??
depiction: Think of intelligence moving from a notebook into a pair of eyes and hands: every idea now has to survive contact with the world. Where the analogy breaks: A successful simulated pick does not prove that real cameras, friction, timing, contact forces, or people will behave like the simulation. The composition follows QUESTION → EXAMPLE → COMPARE → LIMIT and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of What Is Embodied AI?: Embodied AI gives an intelligent system a body—or a simulated body—so its choices must work through sensors, movement, timing, and the physical world.
longDescription: Think of intelligence moving from a notebook into a pair of eyes and hands: every idea now has to survive contact with the world. Where the analogy breaks: A successful simulated pick does not prove that real cameras, friction, timing, contact forces, or people will behave like the simulation. title: Observation & Action Spaces

depiction: Think of the spaces as a checked electrical connector: every pin has a signal, unit, direction, and safe range. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of Observation & Action Spaces: Observation and action spaces are typed interfaces that say exactly what the policy receives and what commands it may send.
longDescription: Think of the spaces as a checked electrical connector: every pin has a signal, unit, direction, and safe range. Worked reference: A seven-degree-of-freedom arm packet contains an RGB image stored as unsigned 8-bit integers with shape [128,128,3], seven joint positions stored as 32-bit floating-point values in radians, and a validity mask. Its action is six pose changes expressed in the tool's coordinate frame plus one gripper command, clipped before a 50 ms hold.

title: Coordinate Frames, Time & Synchronization
learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in Coordinate Frames, Time & Synchronization?
depiction: A frame is graph paper attached to an object; a transform says how one sheet is positioned relative to another at a named time. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of Coordinate Frames, Time & Synchronization: Coordinate frames and timestamps tell the system where a measurement lives and when it was true.
longDescription: A frame is graph paper attached to an object; a transform says how one sheet is positioned relative to another at a named time. Where the analogy breaks: A mathematically valid transform can be wrong because calibration drifted, axes were mislabeled, or samples came from different times. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: Camera sees x=.20 m; its aligned origin is +.50 m in base, and base is +1.00 m in world. Composition gives base x=.70 m and world x=1.70 m. Inverting world→base subtracts 1.00 m and base→camera subtracts .50 m, returning the original camera x=.20 m.

title: Partial Observation in the Physical Loop
learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in Partial Observation in the Physical Loop?
depiction: The controller carries a living case file rather than treating the latest camera frame as the whole world. Where the analogy breaks: Memory can preserve a biased or hallucinated state. In particular, an unobserved grasp break invalidates the rigid-attachment prediction, so confidence without attachment validity, calibration, and recovery tests can make failure harder to detect. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of Partial Observation in the Physical Loop: A physical controller acts from incomplete, delayed, noisy, and sometimes occluded evidence, so it needs memory or a belief about hidden state.
longDescription: The controller carries a living case file rather than treating the latest camera frame as the whole world. Where the analogy breaks: Memory can preserve a biased or hallucinated state. In particular, an unobserved grasp break invalidates the rigid-attachment prediction, so confidence without attachment validity, calibration, and recovery tests can make failure harder to detect. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: A cube is visible at world x=.40 m and is confirmed rigidly grasped. During three occluded steps the applied gripper displacement is +.02 m along world x each time. Under the stated attachment model, the predicted cube mean is $.40+3(.02)=.46$ m and variance grows. If the cube were not grasped, the same gripper motion would not justify moving the cube estimate; that is a different dynamics hypothesis.
boundary: Memory can preserve a biased or hallucinated state. In particular, an unobserved grasp break invalidates the rigid-attachment prediction, so confidence without attachment validity, calibration, and recovery tests can make failure harder to detect.
stageDescriptions: Predict from the applied action through an explicit object–gripper dynamics relation before incorporating the new observation.
Retain uncertainty, attachment validity, and missingness instead of filling every sensor silently.
Test decision recovery after occlusion, delay, broken grasp, and contradictory evidence.
The one-axis rigid-grasp predictor is a deterministic mechanism fixture, not a calibrated tracker or general contact model.

title: Cameras, Proprioception & Sensor Noise

title: Calibration & Spatial Transforms
learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in Calibration & Spatial Transforms?
depiction: Calibration aligns two rulers and writes down both the mapping and how uncertain each mark remains. Where the analogy breaks: Low reprojection error on narrow calibration poses can coexist with large 3-D or workspace-edge error and does not establish mechanical rigidity over time. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of Calibration & Spatial Transforms: Calibration estimates the numeric relationship between sensor readings and physical frames, then records enough uncertainty and revision data to know when that relationship no longer holds.
longDescription: Calibration aligns two rulers and writes down both the mapping and how uncertain each mark remains. Where the analogy breaks: Low reprojection error on narrow calibration poses can coexist with large 3-D or workspace-edge error and does not establish mechanical rigidity over time. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: Fit camera-to-base revision cam-base-v3 on 24 spatial correspondences: training residual is .002 m. Eight held-out edge/depth poses give root-mean-square error .004 m under a .006 m release gate, so v3 is accepted. After a camera bump the held-out error rises to .011 m, so the same revision is invalidated.
boundary: Low reprojection error on narrow calibration poses can coexist with large 3-D or workspace-edge error and does not establish mechanical rigidity over time.
stageDescriptions: Separate camera intrinsics from camera-to-robot extrinsics.
Fit on one pose set and validate on held-out spatial coverage.
Propagate transform uncertainty and trigger recalibration on drift.
Uncertainty arithmetic is illustrative and not a calibrated camera run.

title: Sensor Fusion & Tracking
learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in Sensor Fusion & Tracking?
depiction: Each track is a hypothesis with an uncertainty bubble that motion expands and reliable observations shrink. Where the analogy breaks: A filter can be confidently wrong when motion and sensor models share bias, association is incorrect, or reported covariance is uncalibrated. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of Sensor Fusion & Tracking: Sensor fusion combines complementary evidence over time while a tracker preserves object hypotheses, uncertainty, and identity through noise and dropout.
longDescription: Each track is a hypothesis with an uncertainty bubble that motion expands and reliable observations shrink. Where the analogy breaks: A filter can be confidently wrong when motion and sensor models share bias, association is incorrect, or reported covariance is uncalibrated. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: Prior x=.35/variance .03 plus applied +.05 motion and .01 process variance predicts x=.40/variance .04. In this scalar case $H=1$, so the predicted measurement is also .40. Candidate detections .50 and .90 have innovations .10 and .50; a .25 gate associates .50 and rejects .90. With measurement variance .01, gain is .8, updated mean .48, and variance .008.
boundary: A filter can be confidently wrong when motion and sensor models share bias, association is incorrect, or reported covariance is uncalibrated.
stageDescriptions: Predict before associating and correcting observations.
Use uncertainty-scaled innovation gates rather than nearest point alone.

title: Trajectory Schemas & Dataset Cards

alt: Visual explanation of Trajectory Schemas & Dataset Cards: A trajectory dataset stores complete episodes in a versioned schema so every observation, action, event, split, and use condition can be traced.

title: Action Representations & Chunking
learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in Action Representations & Chunking?
depiction: Choose whether to write one steering correction at a time or a short maneuver, while keeping feedback points explicit. Where the analogy breaks: Longer executed prefixes can smooth motion, but they also delay feedback and can compound one bad prediction. Predicting several futures together does not itself create multimodality: the decoder and training objective must explicitly represent distinct plausible action sequences. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of Action Representations & Chunking: An action representation chooses what the policy predicts—joint commands, end-effector changes, or short action chunks—and therefore changes control frequency, ambiguity, and error recovery.
longDescription: Choose whether to write one steering correction at a time or a short maneuver, while keeping feedback points explicit. Where the analogy breaks: Longer executed prefixes can smooth motion, but they also delay feedback and can compound one bad prediction. Predicting several futures together does not itself create multimodality: the decoder and training objective must explicitly represent distinct plausible action sequences. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: At 20 Hz, H=4 predicts 200 ms of actions. Executing all four open-loop delays visual correction by 200 ms; executing first two replans after 100 ms while retaining the remaining predictions only as unused intent.
boundary: Longer executed prefixes can smooth motion, but they also delay feedback and can compound one bad prediction. Predicting several futures together does not itself create multimodality: the decoder and training objective must explicitly represent distinct plausible action sequences.
stageDescriptions: Compare absolute, delta, joint, and task-space semantics through forward effects.
Align chunk inputs/targets and mask padding across episode ends.
Measure smoothness, latency, compounding error, and recovery as chunk length changes.
Durations and recovery cases are deterministic design fixtures.

title: Build a Behavior-Cloning Policy

alt: Visual explanation of Build a Behavior-Cloning Policy: The behavior-cloning capstone trains a typed trajectory policy and tests whether imitation survives its own closed-loop state distribution.

title: Language Grounding
learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in Language Grounding?
depiction: Translate a verbal request into pointers and testable conditions on the robot's current map. Where the analogy breaks: A correct noun phrase does not imply the object exists, is reachable, or can be manipulated safely; language-model fluency is not grounded evidence. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of Language Grounding: Language grounding connects words such as ‘red cup’ or ‘put inside’ to observable entities, relations, task state, and feasible skills in the current environment.
longDescription: Translate a verbal request into pointers and testable conditions on the robot's current map. Where the analogy breaks: A correct noun phrase does not imply the object exists, is reachable, or can be manipulated safely; language-model fluency is not grounded evidence. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: Instruction ‘place the red cup inside the blue bowl’ yields cup candidates C1/C2; C1 is red with score .9 but unreachable, C2 is orange .4 and reachable. Neither satisfies both language and affordance, so the system abstains rather than choosing one score alone.
boundary: A correct noun phrase does not imply the object exists, is reachable, or can be manipulated safely; language-model fluency is not grounded evidence.
stageDescriptions: Turn language into explicit referents, relations, goals, and constraints.
Separate semantic match from physical affordance and current feasibility.
Ask, abstain, or recover when multiple bindings remain plausible.
The scores are fixed teaching values, not calibrated perception or language outputs.

alt: Visual explanation of Multimodal Policy Encoders: A multimodal policy encoder turns images, language, proprioception, and task state into aligned tokens while preserving modality identity, timing, masks, and geometry.
longDescription: Seat several specialists at one table, give each notes with a name and timestamp, then control who may read whose notes. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: Image yields 64 tokens, language 12, proprioception 1, goal 1: concatenation gives 78 tokens of width 128 and attention score shape [B,H,78,78]; masking one missing proprio token leaves 77 valid keys.

title: Transformer Action Policies
learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in Transformer Action Policies?
depiction: Write a decision timeline where each prediction may read only pages already available at that physical instant, and print the answer on the previous page rather than handing it the answer page. Where the analogy breaks: Correct target shifting prevents one leakage path but does not solve exposure bias, action discretization error, unsupported states, or closed-loop instability. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of Transformer Action Policies: A transformer action policy predicts the next action or action chunk from a causal sequence of grounded observations, instructions, states, and earlier applied actions.
longDescription: Write a decision timeline where each prediction may read only pages already available at that physical instant, and print the answer on the previous page rather than handing it the answer page. Where the analogy breaks: Correct target shifting prevents one leakage path but does not solve exposure bias, action discretization error, unsupported states, or closed-loop instability. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: Serialize `[instruction,o0,a0,o1,a1]`. The hidden state at o0 predicts target a0 from `[instruction,o0]`; the hidden state at o1 predicts a1 from `[instruction,o0,a0,o1]`. With batch 2, five positions, width 32, and four action bins, hidden states are `[2,5,32]`, the attention mask is `[2,1,5,5]`, and logits are `[2,5,4]` before selecting o0/o1 prediction rows. Putting the a1 logit at the a1 token with a diagonal causal mask leaks the answer because that position can read itself.
boundary: Correct target shifting prevents one leakage path but does not solve exposure bias, action discretization error, unsupported states, or closed-loop instability.
stageDescriptions: Specify token order, prediction position, and attention permission for every action target.
Shift targets onto pre-action positions and mask episode padding without crossing episode boundaries.
Compare teacher-forced likelihood with free-running and chunked control.
The token examples are serialization fixtures rather than trained-policy results.

title: Diffusion Policies
learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in Diffusion Policies?
depiction: Sculpt a coherent short maneuver from noise while repeatedly consulting the current scene and task. Where the analogy breaks: Diffusion can represent multiple action modes but does not guarantee they are feasible, safe, grounded, or fast enough for the control deadline. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of Diffusion Policies: A diffusion policy generates an action sequence by repeatedly denoising random action noise while conditioning on the current observation and task.
longDescription: Sculpt a coherent short maneuver from noise while repeatedly consulting the current scene and task. Where the analogy breaks: Diffusion can represent multiple action modes but does not guarantee they are feasible, safe, grounded, or fast enough for the control deadline. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: For one batch, horizon 2, and scalar action, clean chunk [1,−1] has shape [1,2,1]; condition has shape [1,C], where C is the condition-feature width, and timestep has shape [1]. With alpha=.8, sigma=.6, noise [.5,.5], corruption gives [1.1,−.5]. If predicted noise is [.4,.2], one clean estimate is [(1.1−.6×.4)/.8,(−.5−.6×.2)/.8]=[1.075,−.775].
boundary: Diffusion can represent multiple action modes but does not guarantee they are feasible, safe, grounded, or fast enough for the control deadline.
stageDescriptions: Trace action, noise, timestep, condition, and prediction shapes through training.
Separate denoising iterations from real control steps and policy calls.
Measure action multimodality, sampling latency, prefix execution, and recovery.
Corruption and latency values are deterministic teaching fixtures.

title: Hierarchical Skills & Long Horizons
learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in Hierarchical Skills & Long Horizons?
depiction: Plan with named procedures, but require each to declare when it starts, what it promises, and how it stops. alt: Visual explanation of Hierarchical Skills & Long Horizons: A hierarchical controller chooses a reusable skill, supplies arguments, watches termination, and recovers when the skill no longer matches the world.
longDescription: Plan with named procedures, but require each to declare when it starts, what it promises, and how it stops. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: grasp(cup) requires reachable and gripper-empty; after five steps contact is absent and timeout fires. The system records failure, does not mark holding(cup), and routes to reobserve rather than place(cup).
boundary: Named skills can hide brittle controllers, ambiguous effects, and errors that compound across long plans.
stageDescriptions: Specify preconditions, arguments, effects, invariants, and all termination causes.
Keep selection separate from low-level execution evidence.
Replan after violated postconditions or timeouts.
The traces are deterministic state-machine cases.
