title: System Identification & Sim-to-Real
learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in System Identification & Sim-to-Real?
depiction: Measure how the machine responds, tune a simulator range, then test controllers within and beyond that range. Where the analogy breaks: Many parameter combinations can explain the same trajectory, and randomization can hide rather than eliminate structural error. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of System Identification & Sim-to-Real: System identification estimates physical parameters from action-response data, while sim-to-real testing asks whether those estimates and randomized simulations support control under changed dynamics.
longDescription: Measure how the machine responds, tune a simulator range, then test controllers within and beyond that range. Where the analogy breaks: Many parameter combinations can explain the same trajectory, and randomization can hide rather than eliminate structural error. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: Applied force 2 N produces acceleration 1 m/s² in a frictionless fixture, estimating mass 2 kg. With unknown friction, one equation cannot identify mass and friction separately.
boundary: Many parameter combinations can explain the same trajectory, and randomization can hide rather than eliminate structural error.
stageDescriptions: Check identifiability before interpreting fitted values.
Separate calibration trajectories from held-out transfer evaluation.
Report randomized factors, adaptation data, and residual confounds.
The force values are fixtures, not hardware measurements.

title: Recovery, Intervention & Human Control
learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in Recovery, Intervention & Human Control?
depiction: Build the cockpit: learned pilot, instruments, alarms, manual override, and recorder must agree who is in control. Where the analogy breaks: A tested fallback suite cannot prove absence of unknown hazards or authorize physical deployment. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of Recovery, Intervention & Human Control: This capstone operates a learned controller inside feedback, planning, skill, transfer, and independent recovery boundaries, then tests when control must hand off.
longDescription: Build the cockpit: learned pilot, instruments, alarms, manual override, and recorder must agree who is in control. Where the analogy breaks: A tested fallback suite cannot prove absence of unknown hazards or authorize physical deployment. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: A stale camera triggers hold in 20 ms; fallback re-observes for three packets; a human resumes learned control. Completion is assisted recovery, not uninterrupted autonomy.

stageDescriptions: Put runtime authority after every optimizing component.
Inject sensor, model, controller, skill, and dynamics faults separately.
Measure detection, intervention latency, fallback, recovery, and residual risk.
The cases are simulated authority fixtures.

title: Embodied Evaluation Suites
learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in Embodied Evaluation Suites?
depiction: Use a wind-tunnel matrix that changes one operating condition at a time, keeps every failed run, and adds error bars to show how little a small cell can resolve. Where the analogy breaks: A broad simulated suite remains finite, can reflect benchmark design, and does not establish physical deployment validity. A confidence interval quantifies sampling uncertainty under its independent-unit assumptions, not simulator bias or transfer error. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of Embodied Evaluation Suites: An embodied evaluation suite crosses tasks with object, layout, instruction, dynamics, sensor, intervention, and embodiment conditions instead of hiding them in one success rate.
longDescription: Use a wind-tunnel matrix that changes one operating condition at a time, keeps every failed run, and adds error bars to show how little a small cell can resolve. Where the analogy breaks: A broad simulated suite remains finite, can reflect benchmark design, and does not establish physical deployment validity. A confidence interval quantifies sampling uncertainty under its independent-unit assumptions, not simulator bias or transfer error. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: Nominal success 9/10 is 90% with an approximate 95% Wilson interval [.60,.98]; novel-layout 2/10 is 20% with interval [.06,.51]. Equal cell weights give macro success $(.90+.20)/2=.55$. The intervals are wide but non-overlapping in this fixture because .51 is below .60; that descriptive separation does not replace a predeclared paired, stratified, or other effect test. Reporting 90% alone still erases the declared layout-transfer question.
boundary: A broad simulated suite remains finite, can reflect benchmark design, and does not establish physical deployment validity. A confidence interval quantifies sampling uncertainty under its independent-unit assumptions, not simulator bias or transfer error.
stageDescriptions: Cross task capability with explicit shift and intervention axes.
Separate autonomous, assisted, safe, grounded, and deadline-qualified success.
Report cell-level independent-unit denominators, interval method, failures, and aggregate weights.
The rates and Wilson intervals are deterministic calculations from fixed teaching counts, not measured robot capability.

title: Robustness & Generalization
learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in Robustness & Generalization?
depiction: Shake the system inside its operating envelope, then test genuinely new combinations outside the examples it memorized. Where the analogy breaks: Passing enumerated shifts cannot establish open-world generalization or rule out all shortcuts and coupled factors. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of Robustness & Generalization: Robustness tests controlled perturbations near known conditions; generalization tests new objects, layouts, instructions, or dynamics that were withheld by design.
longDescription: Shake the system inside its operating envelope, then test genuinely new combinations outside the examples it memorized. Where the analogy breaks: Passing enumerated shifts cannot establish open-world generalization or rule out all shortcuts and coupled factors. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: Changing table texture leaves task unchanged and should preserve action; swapping requested red to blue is causal and should change target. A policy doing the opposite reveals texture shortcut and weak grounding.
boundary: Passing enumerated shifts cannot establish open-world generalization or rule out all shortcuts and coupled factors.
stageDescriptions: Name whether each change is nuisance, task-relevant, compositional, or out-of-range.
Verify no source or derived window leaks across held-out groups.
Use severity curves and counterfactual pairs, not one average shift.
The shifts are deterministic scenarios, not field statistics.

title: Latency, Safety & Operations
learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in Latency, Safety & Operations?
depiction: Run the controller like a safety-critical service with a stopwatch, circuit breakers, receipts, and a tested previous version. Where the analogy breaks: Simulation timing and software gates do not establish physical stop distance, hardware certification, or complete hazard coverage. A percentile SLO also depends on its load, sample window, clock, and quantile method. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of Latency, Safety & Operations: Operating an embodied policy means every sensed decision and command must arrive before a deadline, stay within independent constraints, and leave enough evidence to stop, recover, and roll back.
longDescription: Run the controller like a safety-critical service with a stopwatch, circuit breakers, receipts, and a tested previous version. Where the analogy breaks: Simulation timing and software gates do not establish physical stop distance, hardware certification, or complete hazard coverage. A percentile SLO also depends on its load, sample window, clock, and quantile method. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: The declared SLO is p99 camera-to-applied-command latency $\le50$ ms under the test load. Observed p50 is 32 ms and p99 is 88 ms. The median describes a typical command and passes 50 ms, but it is not an average and cannot satisfy the tail SLO; p99 exceeds the threshold by 38 ms. The watchdog holds rather than applying late commands.
boundary: Simulation timing and software gates do not establish physical stop distance, hardware certification, or complete hazard coverage. A percentile SLO also depends on its load, sample window, clock, and quantile method.
stageDescriptions: Measure end-to-end age and every stage contribution at tail percentiles against a predeclared SLO.
Define deadline, stale-input, overload, constraint, and watchdog actions.
Test rollback, incident replay, authority, and revision provenance.
Latencies are constructed fixtures, not hardware benchmarks; the p99 estimate assumes the named load and measurement procedure.

title: Reproducible Embodied GPU Runs
learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in Reproducible Embodied GPU Runs?
depiction: Package the simulator, policy, evaluator, and flight recorder as one bounded field kit rather than a notebook that merely produced a curve. Where the analogy breaks: A successful hosted GPU run is external simulation evidence, not a browser execution, physical robot run, or universal architecture result. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of Reproducible Embodied GPU Runs: A reproducible embodied accelerator run pins simulator, data, policy, hardware, seeds, budgets, evaluation matrix, video/trajectory artifacts, and stop rules behind portable smoke and full profiles.
longDescription: Package the simulator, policy, evaluator, and flight recorder as one bounded field kit rather than a notebook that merely produced a curve. Where the analogy breaks: A successful hosted GPU run is external simulation evidence, not a browser execution, physical robot run, or universal architecture result. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: Smoke trains one shared policy for 30 updates with batch 64 and seed 13, then evaluates prefix 1 versus prefix 4 on the same 12 disturbed 24-step episodes. Full uses 3,000 updates, batch 512, seeds 13/29/47/61/83, and 200 paired episodes per seed. The JSON must contain schema_version, execution, config, pairs, invariants, decision, and scope_boundary; no numeric success band or winner is promised before a matching reviewed run exists.
boundary: A successful hosted GPU run is external simulation evidence, not a browser execution, physical robot run, or universal architecture result.
stageDescriptions: Use one provider-neutral command for Colab, compatible GPU services, and local runs.
Separate exact smoke invariants from stochastic full-run observations.
Preserve every episode/seed/cell, video, failure, and provenance record.
The browser classifies evidence and does not execute the external runner.

title: Original Embodied-System Study
learningQuestion: What must happen between “CONTRACT” and “BOUND” in Original Embodied-System Study?
depiction: Complete one small robotics paper whose system, negative cases, and evidence can be independently inspected. Where the analogy breaks: One simulator, embodiment, task matrix, seed set, and budget support only a bounded course-scale conclusion, never a physical deployment claim. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages.
alt: Visual explanation of Original Embodied-System Study: The final capstone reproduces one bounded embodied policy and runs one controlled cross-course intervention across perception, data, policy, planning, control, or operations.
longDescription: Complete one small robotics paper whose system, negative cases, and evidence can be independently inspected. Where the analogy breaks: One simulator, embodiment, task matrix, seed set, and budget support only a bounded course-scale conclusion, never a physical deployment claim. The composition follows CONTRACT → TRANSFORM → OBSERVE → BOUND and keeps the learner's attention on the causal change between stages. Worked reference: Baseline action chunks are reproduced; treatment halves executed prefix under identical data/updates. Recovery improves on layout shift, nominal smoothness falls, and one deadline failure remains, so the decision follows predeclared gates rather than headline success.
boundary: One simulator, embodiment, task matrix, seed set, and budget support only a bounded course-scale conclusion, never a physical deployment claim.
stageDescriptions: Reproduce and accept the working baseline before branching treatment.
Change one mechanism with matched data, interactions, updates, calls, and cells.
Publish all seeds, failures, assistance, costs, limits, and the next falsifying experiment.
The checklist cannot award external scientific validity.
